text:
Once, in a little village tucked between the mountains, there lived a fox named Fang. Fang was clever, graceful, and had the most vibrant red fur. He loved exploring the woods for delicious berries. One day, while picking berries, he heard about an ancient oak tree that whispered secrets from the past.

Curious, Fang visited every evening, listening. One night, he heard the oak tell about a wise owl who once guided an inventor deep into the enchanted forest.

The inventor created wonderful inventions—magic arrows that could shoot diamonds, a hummingbird feeder that never depleted. The forest was enchanted by these gifts.

Fang decided to try an arrow. He flew high into the air, spotted an eagle, and sent a diamond arrow straight into its talon.

The eagle flew away singing about stars.

That night, Fang realized he had a talent. He crafted beautiful objects from glittering stones. Over time, he became famous. People came from far and wide to admire his work.
